attempt: Transfer TICKET63's rule: a stable separator must become an explicit automaton table with transition closure and collision tests before it can support any theorem claim.

bounded_result.automaton_gate: margin separator states need an explicit cutoff-transition automaton
bounded_result.candidate_theorem: A finite Goldbach margin automaton covers every cutoff lift with positive-margin transitions, or a cutoff collision produces a stable exceptional packet.
bounded_result.counterexample_target: two cutoff lifts with the same margin automaton state but conflicting positive-margin labels
bounded_result.frontier_rule: The next useful artifact is an explicit state table plus a proof of closure or a first collision. Heuristic separator stability is not enough.

obstruction: This is a transfer discipline, not a solution. It raises the target from bounded separator survival to automaton closure.
candidate_theorem: A finite Goldbach margin automaton covers every cutoff lift with positive-margin transitions, or a cutoff collision produces a stable exceptional packet.
next_experiment: Build the problem-specific automaton table and quotient/collision audit.
claim_boundary: No goldbach proof and no certified goldbach counterexample.
